metaDescription: {{BRAND_NAME}} supports dark mode — toggle from the navbar or let it follow your system preference. Every panel, sidebar, and table element adapts automatically.
heroHeadline: Work the way your eyes prefer.
heroSubtext: Toggle dark mode from the navbar or let Tablesmit follow your system setting. The entire interface adapts — table, sidebars, panels, and all export previews.

benefits.heading: Follows your system preference
benefits.body: On first visit, Tablesmit reads your system's colour scheme preference. If your OS is set to dark, Tablesmit opens dark — without touching a setting.

benefits.heading: Toggle from the navbar in one click
benefits.body: The sun and moon icon in the navbar switches between light and dark instantly. Your preference is saved and remembered across sessions.

benefits.heading: Every element adapts — including the table
benefits.body: Backgrounds, borders, cell content, sidebar panels, dropdowns, and tooltips all switch correctly. The table grid is readable in both modes — borders stay visible, text stays legible.

benefits.heading: Designed for long sessions
benefits.body: Writing and data work often runs for hours. Dark mode reduces eye strain in low-light environments — especially for late evening research and report work.

steps.heading: Click the moon icon in the navbar
steps.body: The moon icon (dark mode off) and sun icon (dark mode on) sit in the right side of the navbar. Click to toggle.

steps.heading: Or let it follow your system
steps.body: If you have not manually toggled, Tablesmit uses your OS preference — set in System Preferences (Mac) or Display settings (Windows/Linux).

steps.heading: Your preference is remembered
steps.body: The setting is stored in your browser. On your next visit, Tablesmit opens in the mode you last used.

useCases: Researchers working late at night who need a low-glare interface during long analysis sessions
Writers who prefer dark interfaces for extended focus work
Developers and technical users who keep their entire OS in dark mode and want their tools to match
Anyone in a low-light environment where a bright white interface causes strain
